hypothesis: In the ALP reactor simulation (configuration II) with 7200 s experimental time and no QCM-range prior, a coordinated swarm of five LLM agents that are assigned disjoint regions of (B bubbler temperature, B pulse duration) by a simple central allocator will discover and correctly characterize self-limiting A/B saturation—operationally defined as a plateau in per-cycle QCM gain when increasing B pulse beyond 40 s at T_B ≥ 550 K—in at least 60% of runs, compared to ≤20% for an otherwise identical single-agent baseline.
